headline: Microsoft ties a 140-package npm supply-chain attack on the Mastra AI framework to North Korea's Sapphire Sleet, as the FortiBleed credential dump and a fresh Klue OAuth breach keep enterprise identity under siege.

emerging_trends.text: North Korea's Sapphire Sleet keeps weaponizing open-source ecosystems, this time poisoning more than 140 npm packages tied to the Mastra AI framework to reach developer machines.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Mastra AI Supply Chain Hits 140 npm Packages

emerging_trends.text: Stolen tokens and credentials now drive the biggest intrusions, from the 86,644 FortiBleed firewalls to the Klue OAuth tokens that let Icarus loot connected Salesforce environments.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Klue OAuth Breach Draws Icarus Claim

emerging_trends.text: Fresh, low-profile malware keeps surfacing, with Prinz Eugen ransomware skipping ransom notes entirely and CryptoBandits hiding command traffic inside Tor.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Prinz Eugen Ransomware Skips the Note

topics.title: FortiBleed Credential Campaign
topics.latest_developments: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 published a threat brief guiding defenders through mitigating the large-scale credential attacks behind FortiBleed, which has now compromised 86,644 internet-facing FortiGate firewalls and VPN gateways.
topics.summary: FortiBleed, pinned on Russian-speaking actors, exposed credentials on roughly half the internet-reachable Fortinet appliances; CISA and Unit 42 urge operators to rotate credentials and harden devices.

sources.title: Threat Brief: Mitigating Large-Scale Credential Attacks
CISA Warns Fortinet Customers as FortiBleed Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised

topics.title: Mastra AI Supply Chain Hits 140 npm Packages
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft attributed a supply-chain attack that compromised more than 140 npm packages tied to the Mastra AI framework to North Korea's Sapphire Sleet, the group also tracked as BlueNoroff.
topics.summary: Sapphire Sleet, a North Korean state-aligned group, poisoned npm packages linked to the Mastra AI agent framework to plant malware on developers' machines; teams building on Mastra should audit dependencies and rotate any exposed secrets.

sources.title: Microsoft links Mastra AI supply chain attack to North Korean hackers

topics.title: Prinz Eugen Ransomware Skips the Note
topics.latest_developments: A new ransomware operation named Prinz Eugen surfaced that prioritizes recently modified files for encryption and leaves no ransom note on the infected system.
topics.summary: Prinz Eugen targets fresh, high-value files first and drops no note, complicating victim identification and recovery; defenders should watch for rapid encryption of recently edited documents and maintain offline backups.

sources.title: New Prinz Eugen ransomware prioritizes recent files for encryption

topics.title: Macron Presses Democracies on AI Regulation
topics.latest_developments: French President Emmanuel Macron urged the world's wealthy democracies to jointly regulate advanced AI systems and pressed the United States to share its cutting-edge models.
topics.summary: Macron's call frames advanced AI governance as a coordination problem among allied democracies, arriving amid Washington's export controls on frontier models; it signals pressure for shared rules over unilateral restriction.

sources.title: French President Urges US to Share Cutting-Edge AI and Democracies to Cooperate on Regulation

topics.title: Klue OAuth Breach Draws Icarus Claim
topics.latest_developments: A new extortion group calling itself Icarus publicly claimed the Klue breach as the victim list grew, with Klue confirming attackers stole OAuth tokens that linked to customers' Salesforce environments and Salesforce disabling the Klue Battlecards integration.
topics.summary: Market-intelligence platform Klue lost OAuth tokens that let attackers pull data from connected Salesforce instances at customers including Huntress and Recorded Future; affected firms should revoke Klue tokens and review Salesforce access logs.

sources.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
Klue breach lead to Salesforce data theft, Huntress affected
Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack

topics.title: CryptoBandits Malware Abuses Tor
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek detailed CryptoBandits, malware that doubles as a backdoor and routes traffic through a local SOCKS5 proxy over Tor, blending data theft with remote code execution.
topics.summary: CryptoBandits combines an information stealer and a remote-access backdoor, hiding its command channel behind Tor; organizations should flag unexpected local SOCKS5 proxies and Tor traffic from endpoints.

sources.title: CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor

weather: Sunday Night: Slight Chance Rain Showers then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, low 62F.

games.recap: Fulford's pinch 2-run double in 8th lifts Rockies past Pirates 4-3, Freeland reaches 1,000 Ks

business_politics.title: Vance Heads to Iran Talks as Hormuz Closes Again
business_politics.latest_developments: Vice President JD Vance left Saturday for the U.S.-Iran talks now set for Sunday in Switzerland, while Washington and Qatar worked on a plan to free billions in frozen Iranian funds for humanitarian spending and Tehran moved to sell oil on the open market for the first time since 2018.
business_politics.summary: Iran's joint military command declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again over Israeli strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon, days after the interim U.S.-Iran accord reopened the waterway; Israel and Hezbollah then agreed to a renewed ceasefire after four Israeli soldiers and dozens of Lebanese died, leaving the deal that governs Gulf oil flows under early strain.

sources.title: Vance Heads to Peace Talks, After Iran Says It Closed Strait of Hormuz
U.S., Qatar Work to Give Iran Access to Billions of Dollars in Frozen Cash
Iran Can Now Sell Oil. How Fast Can It Ramp Up?

business.title: Geese Police Get North Shore Contract
business.latest_developments: The Sports & Exhibition Authority of Pittsburgh and Allegheny County authorized a six-month agreement worth up to $10,500 with Wild Goose Chase LLC to clear geese from North Shore Riverfront Park.
business.summary: Wild Goose Chase LLC, known as the Geese Police of Western Pennsylvania, will run its trained dogs along North Shore Riverfront Park to deter the geese that foul the riverfront grounds across from downtown Pittsburgh.

sources.title: "Geese Police" returning to Pittsburgh's North Shore for goose control

around_town.title: Lincoln Beach Holds Its First Juneteenth
around_town.latest_developments: The historically Black Lincoln Beach section of Upper Burrell held its first Juneteenth celebration Saturday, June 20, organized by the Rev. Vincent Ponder of Community Baptist Church.
around_town.summary: Lincoln Beach, a longtime Black community in Upper Burrell in the Alle-Kiski Valley, marked Juneteenth for the first time with a gathering led by Community Baptist Church's pastor, the Rev. Vincent Ponder.

sources.title: Lincoln Beach, historically Black Upper Burrell community, holds first Juneteenth celebration

around_town.title: Soaking Rain Brings Monday Impact Day
around_town.latest_developments: Saturday evening's storms fizzled, but WTAE meteorologists held Monday, June 22, as an Impact Day for a soaking rain across western Pennsylvania.
around_town.summary: A round of heavy, prolonged rain will move through the Pittsburgh region Monday, June 22, after only isolated storms passed over the weekend.

sources.title: Evening Storms Fizzle; Impact Day Monday

around_teams.title: Off The Bat on Bill Murphy's Pitching Staff
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette "Off The Bat" column weighed whether Pirates pitching coach Bill Murphy has improved the staff across both the rotation and the bullpen.
around_teams.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates hired Bill Murphy to oversee their pitchers, and the Post-Gazette assessed the early returns on the rotation and a bullpen the team has worked to repair.

sources.title: Off The Bat: Has Bill Murphy helped the Pirates' pitching staff?

around_teams.title: SNR Drive on Breakouts and Trade Candidates
around_teams.latest_developments: On the June 19 SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler broke down ESPN writer Ben Solak's picks for 2025 breakouts and "cliff falls," ran through leaguewide trade candidates, and fielded Steelers questions heading into 2026.
around_teams.summary: The Pittsburgh Steelers' SNR Drive podcast looked ahead to the 2026 season, debating which players around the NFL are poised to rise or fall and which could be moved before the season.

sources.title: Breakouts & Cliff Falls + Steelers Q&A Heading Into 2026 | SNR Drive (June.19)

around_teams.title: Drew Allar Recalls His Draft Call
around_teams.latest_developments: In the newest "Forging Steel," rookie quarterback Drew Allar reflected on the moment the Steelers called to draft him.
around_teams.summary: The Pittsburgh Steelers drafted Penn State quarterback Drew Allar in 2026, and the team's "Forging Steel" series captured his reaction to getting the call.

sources.title: When Drew Allar got the call | Forging Steel

team_usa.title: U.S. Wins World Cup Group D
team_usa.latest_developments: Paraguay's 1-0 win over Turkey on Saturday, June 20, secured the United States the top spot in Group D and a place in the round of 32.
team_usa.summary: The U.S. men's national team, which beat Australia 2-0 in Seattle without the calf-injured Christian Pulisic, clinched first in its group at the 2026 World Cup it co-hosts; Paraguay held on a man down to eliminate a Turkey side that piled up 32 shots without scoring.

sources.title: U.S. clinches WC Group D after Paraguay victory
Pulisic sits, but U.S. finds a way vs. Australia

team_usa.title: Douglass Breaks 50 Freestyle World Record
team_usa.latest_developments: Kate Douglass swam the women's 50-meter freestyle in 23.59 seconds at the TYR Pro Swim Series, bettering the 23.61 mark Sweden's Sarah Sjostrom set in July 2023.
team_usa.summary: The American Olympic champion lowered the world record in swimming's shortest sprint, a marker of form for the U.S. ahead of the next Olympic cycle.

sources.title: Douglass breaks women's 50m freestyle record

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era is nearing its end, building on his exclusive report that OpenAI spent $34 billion against $13.07 billion in revenue and contending that OpenAI and Anthropic are racing to public markets without a path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson and Michael Morton discuss how AI reshapes e-commerce, weighing unfalsifiable bear cases, distribution versus referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responding to a Financial Times interview with the Work AI Institute's Rebecca Hinds about a survey of 6,000 digital workers, Newport argues knowledge work was already dysfunctional before AI arrived.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
